key_insight: Adaptive superpixel grouping reduces large 2D diffraction patterns into interpretable, symmetry-aware features without losing the most informative signal.
engineering_relevance: This preprocessing can dramatically speed up downstream analysis of 4D-STEM and synchrotron diffraction datasets, enabling scalable phase mapping and AI workflows.
method: Group pixels into variable-size superpixels based on variance, with dense sampling in high-information regions.
Compare two clustering strategies: top-down K-means and bottom-up agglomerative clustering.
Accelerate distance-based superpixel construction using Gaussian random projection.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports over 100-fold acceleration for phase mapping with non-negative matrix factorization on a 4D-STEM dataset when superpixels are used.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of distance-based clustering, so overly aggressive reduction could blur weak diffraction features or symmetry details.
paper_title: Using superpixels for interpretable feature reduction in large 2D diffraction datasets
